subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap is an imp with a barbed tail and a bloodstained satchel.
source_quote: An imp (devil) with a barbed tail and a bloodstained satchel

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap was rescued by the party from four ogres in Maerimydra.
source_quote: rescued by the party from four ogres in Maerimydra

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap made a contract with the party to provide information and assistance in exchange for his safety.
source_quote: He made a contract with the party to provide information and assistance in exchange for his safety

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap was brought to the prime material plane by a magic user's mistake.
source_quote: He was brought to the prime material plane by a magic user's mistake

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap has no desire to return to the Nine Hells.
source_quote: having no desire to return to the Nine Hells

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap found loopholes in his previous contracts to remain free on the prime material plane.
source_quote: having found loopholes in his previous contracts to remain free

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap complained about the lack of salt in the party's food.
source_quote: He complained about the lack of salt in the party's food

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap revealed that Hledh is the ruler of Maerimydra.
source_quote: Pipyap revealed that the ruler of Maerimydra is Hledh

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap described Veperyd as a fiendish giant in charge of the Coliseum.
source_quote: He identified the locations of two fiendish giants: one in charge of the Coliseum (Veperyd)

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap revealed that a wizard from Mulmaster has shown up for dinner at Vraxen's house.
source_quote: Pipyap revealed that a wizard from Mulmaster has shown up for dinner at Vraxen's house

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap suggested a plan to cause a distraction by instigating a fight between Vraxen and Veperyd.
source_quote: Pipyap suggested a plan to cause a distraction by instigating a fight between Vraxen and Veperyd

subject: Hledh
fact: Hledh is the Fire Giant Queen who rules Maerimydra.
source_quote: The Fire Giant Queen who rules Maerimydra

subject: Hledh
fact: Hledh resides in the eastern castle of Maerimydra.
source_quote: residing in the eastern castle

subject: Veperyd
fact: Veperyd is a fiendish giant who runs the Coliseum in Maerimydra.
source_quote: A fiendish giant who runs the Coliseum in Maerimydra

subject: Veperyd
fact: Veperyd manages the betting pool at the Coliseum.
source_quote: manages the betting pool

subject: Veperyd
fact: Veperyd has been stealing money from Vraxen.
source_quote: Veperyd has been stealing money from Vraxen

subject: Vraxen
fact: Vraxen is a Yugoloth who resides in The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning.
source_quote: A Yugoloth who resides in The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning

subject: Vraxen
fact: Vraxen possesses the book the party is seeking.
source_quote: He possesses the book the party is seeking

subject: Veris
fact: Veris is a High Inquisitor of Mulmaster.
source_quote: A High Inquisitor of Mulmaster

subject: Veris
fact: Veris has travelled to attend the dinner at Vraxen's residence.
source_quote: who has travelled to attend the dinner at Vraxen's residence

subject: Veris
fact: Veris intends to acquire the book the party seeks.
source_quote: where he intends to acquire the book the party seeks

subject: Veris
fact: Veris is secretly hunting Felkur.
source_quote: Secretly hunting Felkur

subject: Graz'zt
fact: Graz'zt is a demon lord who controls Maerimydra and Hillsfar.
source_quote: A demon lord who controls Maerimydra and Hillsfar

subject: Graz'zt
fact: Graz'zt made a deal with the giants to imbue them with fiendish blood.
source_quote: He made a deal with the giants to imbue them with fiendish blood

subject: Graz'zt
fact: Graz'zt brought in Vraxen to ensure his plans are carried out.
source_quote: brought in Vraxen to ensure his plans are carried out

subject: Graz'zt
fact: Graz'zt's symbol is a six-fingered hand.
source_quote: His symbol is a six-fingered hand

subject: Lolth
fact: Lolth is the Drow goddess whose clerics were unable to commune with her for some time.
source_quote: The Drow goddess whose clerics were unable to commune with her for some time

subject: Lolth
fact: Lolth's powers returned after the Blink event.
source_quote: Her powers returned after 'the Blink'

subject: Lolth
fact: Lolth used the Blink event to escape imprisonment.
source_quote: an event she used to escape imprisonment

subject: Lolth
fact: Lolth is now reestablishing her power and organizing her clerics.
source_quote: She is now reestablishing her power, organizing her clerics

subject: Solom Ned'razak
fact: Solom Ned'razak is a male Drow wizard.
source_quote: A male Drow wizard

subject: Solom Ned'razak
fact: Solom Ned'razak's private office and notes were discovered by the party in the Path of Shadows.
source_quote: whose private office and notes were discovered by the party in the Path of Shadows

subject: Solom Ned'razak
fact: Solom Ned'razak's writings revealed his efforts to subvert Lolth's clerics in Maerimydra.
source_quote: His writings revealed his efforts to subvert Lolth's clerics in Maerimydra

subject: Solom Ned'razak
fact: Solom Ned'razak blamed powerful wizards and liches from Menzoberranzan for the Blink.
source_quote: He blamed powerful wizards and liches from Menzoberranzan for the Blink

subject: Solom Ned'razak
fact: Solom Ned'razak initially sought accommodation with demons but realized they only caused chaos and destruction.
source_quote: He initially sought accommodation with demons but realized they only caused chaos and destruction

subject: Owlbear
fact: Owlbear is a loyal companion to the party.
source_quote: A loyal companion to the party

subject: Owlbear
fact: Owlbear participated in combat against ogres at the Black Ember Outpost and in Maerimydra.
source_quote: who participated in combat against ogres at the Black Ember Outpost and in Maerimydra

subject: Owlbear
fact: Owlbear helped Bramgrim and Felkur stay afloat in the deep water by allowing them to ride on its back.
source_quote: Helped Bramgrim and Felkur stay afloat in the deep water by allowing them to ride on its back

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is a former drow metropolis now a city of fire and ash.
source_quote: A former drow metropolis now a city of fire and ash

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is controlled by demonic evil forces aligned with Graz'zt.
source_quote: It is controlled by demonic evil forces aligned with Graz'zt

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is inhabited by fire giants, ogres, hellhounds, drow slaves, and demons.
source_quote: It is inhabited by fire giants, ogres, hellhounds, drow slaves, and demons

subject: Contract negotiation
fact: The party negotiated a contract for Pipyap's services after rescuing him.
source_quote: the party negotiated a contract for his services

subject: Contract negotiation
fact: The party rejected Pipyap's initial standard contract.
source_quote: The party rejected the initial contract

subject: Contract negotiation
fact: Felkur and Bramgrim successfully crafted a favorable contract with Pipyap.
source_quote: Felkur and Bramgrim successfully crafted a favorable contract with Pipyap

subject: Feeding Pipyap
fact: The party fed Pipyap.
source_quote: The party fed Pipyap

subject: Plan formulation
fact: The party decided to prioritize getting the fiendish giant first, then provoking the war, then stealing the book.
source_quote: They decided to prioritize getting the fiendish giant first, then provoking the war, then stealing the book

subject: Healing
fact: The party decided to use second wind to heal Daein and Felkur to full health.
source_quote: The party decided to use second wind to heal Daein and Felkur to full health

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: Black Ember Outpost is a fortress built into a cave wall in the Underdark.
source_quote: A fortress built into a cave wall in the Underdark

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: Black Ember Outpost was made of polished obsidian.
source_quote: made of polished obsidian

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: Black Ember Outpost served as a base for fire giants, ogres, and hellhounds.
source_quote: It served as a base for fire giants, ogres, and hellhounds

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: The party previously cleared out the Black Ember Outpost during their assault.
source_quote: The party previously cleared it out during their assault

subject: Storeroom
fact: The storeroom is a room within the Black Ember Outpost where ogres were hauling water from a well.
source_quote: A room within the Black Ember Outpost where ogres were hauling water from a well

subject: Storeroom
fact: The storeroom contained a hidden chest with treasures.
source_quote: It contained a hidden chest with treasures

subject: Storeroom
fact: The storeroom served as the entry point to the Path of Shadows.
source_quote: served as the entry point to the Path of Shadows

subject: Well
fact: The well is in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: A well in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Well
fact: The well is recognized as the entrance to the Path of Shadows.
source_quote: recognized as the entrance to the Path of Shadows

subject: Path of Shadows
fact: The Path of Shadows is an underground passage of dark, murky, warm water about five feet deep.
source_quote: An underground passage of dark, murky, warm water about five feet deep

subject: Path of Shadows
fact: The Path of Shadows is fed by a stream connecting to a larger lake of shadows.
source_quote: fed by a stream connecting to a larger lake of shadows

subject: Path of Shadows
fact: The Path of Shadows features a five-foot-wide tunnel that the party swam through for about 40 feet.
source_quote: It features a five-foot-wide tunnel that the party swam through for about 40 feet

subject: Path of Shadows
fact: The Path of Shadows leads to Solom Ned'razak's private office and eventually Maerimydra.
source_quote: leading to Solom Ned'razak's private office and eventually Maerimydra

subject: Solom Ned'razak's Office
fact: Solom Ned'razak's Office is a private office area within a large cave along the Path of Shadows.
source_quote: A private office area within a large cave along the Path of Shadows

subject: Solom Ned'razak's Office
fact: Solom Ned'razak's Office contains a lamp and papers detailing Solom's research.
source_quote: containing a lamp and papers detailing Solom's research and observations

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is nestled in a titanic cavern 600 feet tall at its highest point.
source_quote: nestled in a titanic cavern 600 feet tall at its highest point

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra features huge buildings and plateaus 100 feet high connected by 100-foot-wide grand stairs.
source_quote: The city features huge buildings, plateaus 100 feet high connected by 100-foot-wide grand stairs

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra has a thick smoke layer above 250 feet.
source_quote: a thick smoke layer above 250 feet

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is lit by numerous burning braziers creating a heavy charcoal scent.
source_quote: lit by numerous burning braziers creating a heavy charcoal scent

subject: Six-Fingered Courtyard
fact: The Six-Fingered Courtyard is a courtyard on a raised plateau in the western section of Maerimydra.
source_quote: A courtyard on a raised plateau in the western section of Maerimydra

subject: Six-Fingered Courtyard
fact: The Six-Fingered Courtyard is surrounded by black spires bearing a prominent six-fingered symbol.
source_quote: surrounded by black spires bearing a prominent six-fingered symbol

subject: Six-Fingered Courtyard
fact: Demons and giants gather at the Six-Fingered Courtyard to worship a demon lord.
source_quote: Demons and giants gather here to worship a demon lord

subject: Coliseum
fact: The Coliseum is a massive coliseum dominating the southern end of the residential district in Maerimydra.
source_quote: A massive coliseum dominating the southern end of the residential district in Maerimydra

subject: Coliseum
fact: Great gladiator fights occur at the Coliseum.
source_quote: where great gladiator fights occur

subject: Eastern Castle
fact: The Eastern Castle is a castle on a plateau in the eastern side of Maerimydra.
source_quote: A castle on a plateau in the eastern side of Maerimydra

subject: Eastern Castle
fact: The Eastern Castle has two towers stretching from floor to ceiling.
source_quote: with two towers stretching from floor to ceiling

subject: Eastern Castle
fact: The Fire Giant Queen Hledh resides in the Eastern Castle.
source_quote: The Fire Giant Queen Hledh resides here

subject: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning
fact: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning is a tower in Maerimydra where Vraxen resides.
source_quote: A tower in Maerimydra where Vraxen (the Yugoloth) resides

subject: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning
fact: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning contains the book the party seeks and a teleportation circle.
source_quote: It contains the book the party seeks and a teleportation circle

subject: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning
fact: A dinner event is scheduled to occur at The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning.
source_quote: A dinner event is scheduled to occur here

subject: Potion of Flying
fact: A Potion of Flying is a magical potion found in a chest beneath a ragged blanket in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: A magical potion found in a chest beneath a ragged blanket in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Potion of Fire Giant Strength
fact: A Potion of Fire Giant Strength is a magical potion found in a chest beneath a ragged blanket in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: A magical potion found in a chest beneath a ragged blanket in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Coral Gemstones
fact: Coral Gemstones are gemstones found in a chest beneath a ragged blanket in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: Gemstones found in a chest beneath a ragged blanket in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Coral Gemstones
fact: The Coral Gemstones are valued at a total of 400 gold pieces.
source_quote: valued at a total of 400 gold pieces

subject: Solom Ned'razak's Notes
fact: Solom Ned'razak's Notes are papers found in Solom Ned'razak's private office.
source_quote: Papers found in Solom Ned'razak's private office

subject: Solom Ned'razak's Notes
fact: Solom Ned'razak's Notes contain his research and theories on the 'Blink' event.
source_quote: containing his research, theories on the 'Blink' event in the faerzress

subject: Contract with Pipyap
fact: The Contract with Pipyap is a formal agreement negotiated between the party and the imp Pipyap.
source_quote: A formal agreement negotiated between the party and the imp Pipyap

subject: The Book
fact: The Book is a specific book that Maram il Vass wants the party to steal from Vraxen.
source_quote: A specific book that Maram il Vass wants the party to steal from Vraxen

subject: The Book
fact: The Book is located in The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning.
source_quote: located in The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning

subject: Pipyap's Satchel
fact: Pipyap's Satchel is a bloodstained satchel with an oversized button carried by the imp Pipyap.
source_quote: A bloodstained satchel with an oversized button, carried by the imp Pipyap

subject: Pipyap's Satchel
fact: Pipyap desperately tried to keep hold of his satchel while being tormented by ogres.
source_quote: He desperately tried to keep hold of it while being tormented by ogres

subject: Mage Hand
fact: Mage Hand was used by Felkur to tactically strip the ill-fitting uniforms off ogres during the assault on the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: Used by Felkur to tactically strip the ill-fitting uniforms off ogres during the assault on the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Fireball
fact: Fireball was cast by Felkur to initiate combat against ogres in the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: Cast by Felkur to initiate combat against ogres in the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Fireball
fact: Fireball was cast by Felkur against ogres tormenting Pipyap in Maerimydra.
source_quote: and later against ogres tormenting Pipyap in Maerimydra

subject: Auditory Illusions
fact: Auditory Illusions were used by Daein to create the vocalization of a female hellhound to lure male hellhounds into distraction at the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: Used by Daein to create the vocalization of a female hellhound to lure male hellhounds into distraction at the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Auditory Illusions
fact: Auditory Illusions were used by Daein to mimic a fire giant's voice to convince the rest of the outpost that the giants had won the battle.
source_quote: and later to mimic a fire giant's voice to convince the rest of the outpost that the giants had won the battle

subject: Spirit Guardians
fact: Spirit Guardians was cast by Bramgrim to engage in combat during the Black Ember Outpost assault.
source_quote: Cast by Bramgrim to engage in combat during the Black Ember Outpost assault

subject: Spirit Guardians
fact: Bramgrim lost concentration on Spirit Guardians after being struck by an ogre in the storeroom.
source_quote: He waded into the fray but lost concentration after being struck by an ogre in the storeroom

subject: Silence
fact: Silence was cast by Bramgrim to prevent alarms from being raised at the well in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost.
source_quote: Cast by Bramgrim to prevent alarms from being raised at the well in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost

subject: Mass Healing Word
fact: Mass Healing Word was cast by Bramgrim during the storeroom combat to provide healing to the party.
source_quote: Cast by Bramgrim during the storeroom combat to provide healing to the party

subject: Mass Healing Word
fact: Bramgrim dealt 27 points of necrotic damage while casting Mass Healing Word.
source_quote: also dealing 27 points of necrotic damage

subject: Hunter's Mark
fact: Hunter's Mark was used by Akritas to mark targets and enhance her attacks against ogres.
source_quote: Used by Akritas to mark targets and enhance her attacks against ogres

subject: Protective Field
fact: Protective Field was used by Daein to reduce damage from an ogre's attack.
source_quote: Used by Daein to reduce damage from an ogre's attack

subject: Protective Field
fact: Protective Field reduced 13 points of damage to 6 by rolling 1d8 plus 1.
source_quote: reducing 13 points of damage to 6 by rolling 1d8 plus 1

subject: Firebolt
fact: Firebolt was used by Felkur to deliver the finishing blow to the last remaining ogre during the encounter with Pipyap in Maerimydra.
source_quote: A cantrip used by Felkur to deliver the finishing blow to the last remaining ogre during the encounter with Pipyap in Maerimydra

subject: Second Wind
fact: Second Wind was used by Daein and Felkur to restore their hit points to full health before proceeding with their mission in Maerimydra.
source_quote: Used by Daein and Felkur to restore their hit points to full health before proceeding with their mission in Maerimydra

subject: Dinner deadline
fact: The party must steal the book before the dinner concludes and Veris obtains it.
source_quote: they must steal the book before the dinner concludes and he obtains it

subject: Distraction plan
fact: The party plans to start a fight between Vraxen and Veperyd about Veperyd stealing from Vraxen's bets.
source_quote: Start fight between Vraxen and Veperyd about Veperyd stealing from Vraxen's bets
